children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall administer the recreation area in accordance with applicable provisions of this Act and the provisions of law generally applicable to units of the national park system, including the Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved (; , 2–4), and the Act of (). In the administration of such recreation area, the Secretary may utilize such statutory authority as may be available to him for the protection of natural and cultural resources as he deems necessary to carry out the purposes of this Act. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to amend or alter the responsibilities of the International Boundary and Water Commission, United States and Mexico, under any applicable treaty.August 25, 1916August 7, 194639 Stat. 53516 U.S.C. 1160 Stat. 8851See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Compliance with treaties and other commitments or agreements
children.label: b Compliance with treaties and other commitments or agreements

children.text: the demarcation and maintenance of boundaries;
the use, storage, and furnishing of water;
investigations relative to the operation of the Amistad Dam; and

children.heading: Survey of cultural resources; report to Congress
children.label: c Survey of cultural resources; report to Congress
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a survey of the cultural resources in the immediate vicinity of the recreation area. The Secretary is authorized to enter into cooperative agreements with public or private entities, including landowners, for the purpose of conducting the survey required by this subsection. Not later than two years after , the Secretary shall submit a report to the Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate on the results of the survey required by this subsection.November 28, 1990

The Secretary shall permit hunting and fishing on lands and waters under the Secretary’s jurisdiction within the recreation area in accordance with applicable Federal and State law. The Secretary may designate zones where, and establish periods when, hunting or fishing will not be permitted for reasons of public safety, administration, fish and wildlife management, or public use and enjoyment.

Except in emergencies any regulations issued by the Secretary under this subsection shall be put into effect only after consultation with the appropriate State agencies responsible for hunting and fishing activities.

children.heading: Rescue, firefighting, and law enforcement assistance
children.label: e Rescue, firefighting, and law enforcement assistance
children.text: For purposes of administering the recreation area, the Secretary may enter into cooperative agreements with any Federal agency, the State of Texas, or any political subdivision thereof, for the rendering, on a reimbursable basis, of rescue, firefighting, and law enforcement and fire preventive assistance.
